informal_statement.text: Given two integer slices a and b, return the length of the longest sequence of integers that appears as a (not necessarily contiguous) subsequence of both a and b, in the same relative order.
preconditions: a and b are slices of i32 values of any length
required_properties: the result is between 0 and min(a.len(), b.len()) inclusive
there exists a sequence of indices i_1 < i_2 < ... < i_k into a and j_1 < j_2 < ... < j_k into b with a[i_m] == b[j_m] for each m and k equal to the result
no strictly longer such common subsequence exists
edge_cases: a and b share no elements (result is 0)
one input is a subsequence of the other
repeated elements within a single input

semantic_units.description: Range bound: the result is in [0, min(len(a), len(b))].
common_failure_modes: stating only an upper bound of min(len(a), len(b))

semantic_units.description: Existential witness: there exist strictly increasing index sequences into a and b of equal length k pointing to equal values.
common_failure_modes: omitting the strict-increase condition on indices
comparing values element-wise across slices of different lengths

semantic_units.description: Maximality: no common subsequence strictly longer than the returned length exists.
common_failure_modes: confusing maximality with uniqueness of witness

semantic_units.description: Contiguous vs subsequence distinction: the definition allows non-contiguous index selections.
common_failure_modes: solving the longest common substring instead of subsequence

semantic_units.description: Symmetry: lcsLength a b = lcsLength b a.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (a b : Arr), lcsLength a b ≤ a.length ∧ lcsLength a b ≤ b.length
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The result is bounded above by the lengths of both inputs.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (a b : Arr), ∃ (ia ib : List Nat), CommonSubseq a b ia ib ∧ ia.length = lcsLength a b
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a common subsequence of length equal to the returned value.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (a b : Arr) (ia ib : List Nat), CommonSubseq a b ia ib → ia.length ≤ lcsLength a b
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: No common subsequence is strictly longer than the returned value.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (a b : Arr), lcsLength a b = lcsLength b a
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: LCS length is symmetric in its arguments.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_lcs_witness_indices (a b : List Int) :
  let k := lcs_length a b
  ? ia ib : List Nat, ia.length = k ? ib.length = k ? StrictlyIncreasing ia ? StrictlyIncreasing ib ? (? t : Nat, t < k ? a[ia.get! t]! = b[ib.get! t]!)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Witness via strictly increasing index selections (non-contiguous subsequence semantics).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_lcs_maximality (a b : List Int) :
  let k := lcs_length a b
  ? m : Nat, (? ia ib : List Nat, ia.length = m ? ib.length = m ? StrictlyIncreasing ia ? StrictlyIncreasing ib ? (? t : Nat, t < m ? a[ia.get! t]! = b[ib.get! t]!)) ? m = k
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No common subsequence defined by increasing indices is longer than returned length.
